verses.2: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold.
verses.3: I am weary with my crying.
verses.4: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head.
verses.6: Don’t let those who wait for you be shamed through me, Lord GOD of Armies.
verses.7: Because for your sake, I have borne reproach.
verses.8: I have become a stranger to my brothers,
verses.9: For the zeal of your house consumes me.
verses.10: When I wept and I fasted,
verses.11: When I made sackcloth my clothing,
verses.12: Those who sit in the gate talk about me.
verses.13: But as for me, my prayer is to you, LORD, in an acceptable time.
verses.14: Deliver me out of the mire, and don’t let me sink.
verses.15: Don’t let the flood waters overwhelm me,
verses.16: Answer me, LORD, for your loving kindness is good.
verses.17: Don’t hide your face from your servant,
verses.18: Draw near to my soul and redeem it.
verses.19: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor.
verses.20: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am full of heaviness.
verses.21: They also gave me poison for my food.
verses.22: Let their table before them become a snare.
verses.23: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they can’t see.
verses.24: Pour out your indignation on them.
verses.26: For they persecute him whom you have wounded.
verses.27: Charge them with crime upon crime.
verses.28: Let them be blotted out of the book of life,
verses.29: But I am in pain and distress.
verses.30: I will praise the name of God with a song,
verses.31: It will please the LORD better than an ox,
verses.32: The humble have seen it, and are glad.
verses.33: For the LORD hears the needy,
verses.34: Let heaven and earth praise him;
verses.35: For God will save Zion, and build the cities of Judah.
verses.36: The children also of his servants shall inherit it.
